title: Opaque payload and the aion_kit package skeleton
purpose: Establishes the cluster's foundation and its keystone: the aion_kit Gleam package skeleton (manifest + declaration-only root) and the opaque sealed-payload mechanism that lets a deterministic workflow carry an activity result it never opens (ADR-012). The TYPE a workflow holds — Sealed — is added to the authoring SDK aion_flow (workflow code imports it); the HELPERS that produce and read it — seal, raw, peek — land in aion_kit/payload (worker-side primitives). The split is the design's central boundary call (see solution): hold-and-forward is workflow vocabulary, open-and-decode is activity vocabulary, and the line between them is the determinism boundary. This is the only intra-cluster dependency: KIT-002 (template) and KIT-003 (json) build on this skeleton. brief_dev is NOT reshaped here — that is RM-023; this brief ships and proves the mechanism with its own tests.
task: Create the gleam/aion_kit package: gleam.toml (depending on aion_flow, gleam_stdlib, gleam_json; dev-dep gleeunit; matching the gleam/aion_flow manifest shape exactly — erlang target, licences array, github repository) and the declaration-only root module src/aion_kit.gleam (a PackageRoot marker, matching aion_flow.gleam and aion_client.gleam). Add the Sealed pass-through type to aion_flow at src/aion/payload.gleam: an opaque type wrapping a content-type tag (String) and the raw payload bytes (String — one binary, not a decoded structure), with a constructor and the two accessors content_type and raw_bytes, and NO accessor that returns a decoded view. Create src/aion_kit/payload.gleam with the helpers over aion_flow's Sealed: seal(value, encoder, content_type) building a Sealed from a typed value via a caller-supplied json encoder and explicit content-type (no default content-type — ADR-001); raw(sealed) returning the opaque bytes and content_type(sealed) returning the tag for a consuming activity to decode with its own decoder; peek(sealed, decoder) decoding ONLY a caller-supplied thin facts view (the ADR-012 facts projection over a Sealed), ignoring unnamed fields, returning Result(view, codec.DecodeError) — typed failure, never a panic. Write the tests: gleam/aion_flow/test/payload_test.gleam (Sealed construction, accessors, and the hold-without-decode property — seal a value, forward only raw bytes, decode on the far side) and gleam/aion_kit/test/payload_test.gleam (seal/raw/peek round-trip, and peeking a small facts view from a sealed report far larger than the view). In scope: the package skeleton, the Sealed type, the payload helpers, their tests, and the purity/boundary guarantees C17/C18 for the files this brief introduces. Out of scope: template (KIT-002), json wrangling (KIT-003), and any reshape of brief_dev or its prompts.gleam/enrich.gleam (RM-023).

requirements.title: aion_kit package skeleton
requirements.spec: Create gleam/aion_kit/gleam.toml and gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit.gleam. THE SYSTEM SHALL declare the package name 'aion_kit', target 'erlang', licences ["MIT", "Apache-2.0"], and the github repository { type, user: "tomWhiting", repo: "aion" }, matching the gleam/aion_flow manifest shape. THE SYSTEM SHALL list dependencies gleam_stdlib (>= 0.34.0 and < 2.0.0), gleam_json (>= 2.0.0 and < 4.0.0), and aion_flow, and dev-dependency gleeunit (>= 1.0.0 and < 2.0.0). The root module SHALL contain only module documentation and a declaration-only PackageRoot marker type — no functions, no re-exports of submodules. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT add a dependency on any worker crate, the norn driver, or any agent/process-spawning runtime (ADR-011).
acceptance: gleam/aion_kit/gleam.toml sets name = "aion_kit" and target = "erlang".
The [dependencies] table lists gleam_stdlib, gleam_json, and aion_flow; the [dev-dependencies] table lists gleeunit; no other dependency is present.
src/aion_kit.gleam defines exactly one public type PackageRoot with a single PackageRoot constructor and no public functions.
grep -i 'norn' gleam/aion_kit/gleam.toml produces no matches.
cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam build exits 0.

requirements.title: The Sealed pass-through type in aion_flow
requirements.spec: Create gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam defining the Sealed type a workflow holds and threads between activities without decoding. THE SYSTEM SHALL model Sealed as an opaque type carrying a content-type tag (String) and the raw payload bytes (String). THE SYSTEM SHALL expose a constructor that takes a content-type and bytes, an accessor content_type returning the tag, and an accessor raw_bytes returning the bytes. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose any accessor, method, or field that returns a decoded structured view of the bytes — holding a Sealed costs one binary on the workflow heap, never a decoded nested structure (ADR-012, CN3). The module SHALL NOT import aion/workflow, aion/activity, aion/query, or aion/signal, SHALL NOT perform IO, and SHALL contain no panic, assert, or let-assert.
acceptance: src/aion/payload.gleam defines an opaque Sealed type; for s = the constructor called with content_type "application/json" and bytes "{\"k\":1}", content_type(s) == "application/json" and raw_bytes(s) == "{\"k\":1}".
The module exposes no function returning a decoded view of the bytes (only content_type and raw_bytes read the contents, both returning String).
grep -E 'import aion/(workflow|activity|query|signal)' gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam produces no matches.
grep -E 'panic|let assert|assert ' gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam produces no matches in library code.
cd gleam/aion_flow && gleam build exits 0.

requirements.title: seal, raw, and content_type helpers
requirements.spec: In gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/payload.gleam, build the construction and forwarding helpers over aion_flow's Sealed. WHEN seal(value, encoder, content_type) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL encode value to a JSON string via the caller-supplied encoder and wrap it in a Sealed tagged with the caller-supplied content_type. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT supply a default content-type — the caller passes it explicitly (ADR-001). WHEN raw(sealed) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the Sealed's opaque bytes so a consuming activity decodes them with its own typed decoder; content_type(sealed) SHALL return the tag. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT decode the bytes inside seal or raw. The module SHALL NOT import aion/workflow, aion/activity, aion/query, or aion/signal, and SHALL contain no panic, assert, or let-assert.
acceptance: For a value encoded by a json encoder producing "{\"hello\":\"world\"}", seal(value, encoder, "application/json") yields a Sealed s with raw(s) == "{\"hello\":\"world\"}" and content_type(s) == "application/json".
A round-trip test seals a value, takes raw(s), decodes that String with the matching decoder, and recovers the original value — proving the activity-to-activity forwarding path (S3).
seal takes the content-type as an explicit argument; there is no overload or branch that fills in a content-type when one is not provided.
grep -E 'import aion/(workflow|activity|query|signal)' gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/payload.gleam produces no matches.
cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam test runs the payload suite and the seal/raw round-trip case passes.

requirements.title: peek: a thin facts view from a Sealed
requirements.spec: Add peek to gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/payload.gleam. WHEN peek(sealed, decoder) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL decode ONLY the caller-supplied thin facts view from the Sealed's bytes and SHALL ignore every field the view's decoder does not name (the ADR-012 facts projection over a Sealed: the workflow reads the few fields it needs to route control flow — pass/fail, blocked, changed files, drift — without materialising the whole report). IF the bytes do not satisfy the thin decoder, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return a typed codec.DecodeError and SHALL NOT panic. peek SHALL NOT materialise or return any field outside the supplied view, and SHALL be pure (no IO, no clock, no entropy) so a workflow may call it across the determinism boundary (P3, invariant 2).
acceptance: Given a Sealed whose bytes are a JSON object with ten fields, peek(sealed, decoder) where decoder names only two of them returns Ok of a two-field view and the other eight fields are absent from the result.
peek(sealed, decoder) on bytes that are not valid JSON, or that lack a field the decoder requires, returns Error(codec.DecodeError(...)) — verified by asserting the Error branch, with no panic.

A test seals a report value far larger than the facts view (e.g. a record with a long string field and a list) and peeks a small facts view (e.g. a single boolean and one string) successfully (C8, S6).
grep -E 'panic|let assert' gleam/aion_kit/src/aion_kit/payload.gleam produces no matches.
cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam test runs the payload suite and the peek-ignores-the-rest case passes.

requirements.title: Hold-without-decode property test on the aion_flow side
requirements.spec: Create gleam/aion_flow/test/payload_test.gleam proving the Sealed type's hold-and-forward property from the workflow's vantage. THE SYSTEM SHALL construct a Sealed, read its content_type and raw_bytes, and demonstrate that forwarding the Sealed (or its bytes) to a decoder on the far side recovers the value — WITHOUT the Sealed type exposing a decoded view at any point. THE SYSTEM SHALL assert that the only contents-reading surface is content_type and raw_bytes (both String-returning). The test SHALL NOT import aion_kit (the aion_flow package must prove its own type without depending downward).
acceptance: The test constructs a Sealed with bytes "{\"changed\":3}", asserts raw_bytes equals that string and content_type equals the tag, then decodes the bytes with a json decoder and asserts the recovered value — the Sealed never yielding a decoded view itself.
grep -E 'import aion_kit' gleam/aion_flow/test/payload_test.gleam produces no matches.
cd gleam/aion_flow && gleam test runs payload_test and it passes.
cd gleam/aion_flow && gleam format --check reports no changes for the new module and test.

requirements.title: Skeleton builds, gates clean, and the boundary holds
requirements.spec: Wire the package so it builds, tests, and formats clean, and verify the cluster-wide purity and boundary guarantees for the files this brief introduces. THE SYSTEM SHALL ensure cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam build, gleam test, and gleam format --check all succeed against the aion_flow dependency. THE SYSTEM SHALL ensure no file in gleam/aion_kit/src nor gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam imports aion/workflow, aion/activity, aion/query, aion/signal, references 'norn', or spawns any process/port (CN1, ADR-011), and SHALL ensure library code in those files contains no panic, assert, or let-assert and reads no wall clock, entropy, or IO (CN2, CN7). This requirement carries C17 and C18 for the cluster; KIT-002 and KIT-003 re-verify the same properties for the files they add via their own verification steps (split noted in this task and theirs).
acceptance: cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam build && gleam test && gleam format --check all exit 0.
grep -rE 'import aion/(workflow|activity|query|signal)' gleam/aion_kit/src gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam produces no matches.
grep -ri 'norn' gleam/aion_kit/src gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam produces no matches.
grep -rE 'panic|let assert|spawn|erlang:open_port|process' gleam/aion_kit/src gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam produces no matches in library code (test modules excluded).
Each source file under gleam/aion_kit/src and gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam is at or under 500 code lines (excluding comments and whitespace).

boundaries: SHALL NOT create aion_kit/template.gleam or any text-composition primitive — that is KIT-002.
SHALL NOT create aion_kit/json.gleam or any project/merge/pluck helper — that is KIT-003.
SHALL NOT modify, reshape, or rewire examples/stacked-dev (brief_dev, prompts.gleam, enrich.gleam, activities, locals) — consuming the toolkit is RM-023.
SHALL NOT re-export, wrap, or alias aion_client's Payload or brief_dev's bespoke helpers — aion_kit is the replacement, not a shim (ADR-002).
SHALL NOT add a default content-type, a size or depth cap, or any hardcoded bound to Sealed, seal, raw, or peek (ADR-001).
SHALL NOT add an accessor or function that returns a decoded structured view of a Sealed's bytes (CN3) — only raw, content_type, and the caller-decoder peek read the contents.
SHALL NOT add a dependency on a worker crate, the norn driver, or any agent/process-spawning runtime (ADR-011).
verification: cd gleam/aion_kit && gleam build && gleam test && gleam format --check all exit 0.
cd gleam/aion_flow && gleam build && gleam test && gleam format --check all exit 0 (the new src/aion/payload.gleam and test/payload_test.gleam build and run).
grep -rE 'import aion/(workflow|activity|query|signal)' gleam/aion_kit/src gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam produces no matches.
grep -ri 'norn' gleam/aion_kit/src gleam/aion_flow/src/aion/payload.gleam produces no matches.
The Sealed type exposes only content_type and raw_bytes as contents-reading surface; peek lives in aion_kit/payload and takes the decoder from the caller (no decoded view escapes the type).
A round-trip test proves seal -> raw -> caller-decode recovers the original value, and a peek test proves a small view decodes out of a far larger sealed report.
